name: Past Tense (pretérito perfeito)
summary: The everyday past tense for completed actions, formed by changing the verb ending.
explanation:
The pretérito perfeito is the main past tense for completed actions. Unlike English, it is a single word with its own endings, not a helper plus participle.

For -ar verbs, the endings are -ei (eu), -ou (você/ele/ela), -amos (nós), -aram (vocês/eles/elas). So falar gives falei, falou, falamos, falaram.

For -er and -ir verbs the endings are -i, -eu/-iu, -emos/-imos, -eram/-iram: comer gives comi, comeu; partir gives parti, partiu. Key irregulars include ser/ir (fui, foi), ter (tive, teve), and fazer (fiz, fez).
pattern: stem + ei / ou / amos / aram (for -ar verbs)
commonMistakes: Building the past with a helper verb. Portuguese uses one-word endings: falei, not 'tenho falado' for simple past.
Confusing the -ar nós form (falamos) which looks identical in present and past; context decides.
Forgetting the strong irregulars: ser and ir share fui, foi.
Using the -ar endings on -er verbs (comou instead of comeu).
Dropping the accent on forms that need it, like the eu -i ending.
tip: Master the three regular patterns first, then memorise the high-frequency irregulars fui, tive, fiz, and disse.
